title: License-text snapshot capture in provenance flow
context: The license gate (gate-003) requires a committed license-text snapshot (committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback URL) per source and per reused asset. Currently this is a manual step; a small automation would make the provenance flow more reliable and faster. The tool must retrieve the license text from a URL, compute its SHA-256, submit it to the Wayback Machine for preservation, and commit the result as a structured provenance artifact.
objective: Implement a CLI tool that retrieves a license text URL, computes its SHA-256 hash, captures a Wayback Machine save URL, and writes the snapshot artifact for inclusion in provenance records.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts a license URL as input; fetches the license text; computes SHA-256 of the fetched content.
Submits the URL to the Wayback Machine save API and records the returned permalink.
Writes a structured JSON snapshot artifact: { url, retrievedAt, sha256, waybackUrl, textSnippet } suitable for inclusion in provenance records.
Ships tests with mocked HTTP responses; CI green.
Code is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commits DCO signed-off.
output: A MIT-licensed CLI tool (Node package) for capturing license-text snapshots with SHA-256 + Wayback URL, integrated into the provenance flow.
